summary: How to find information in OFS: follow _refs graph traversal instead of brute-force grep. Progressive disclosure in 4 steps.

protocol.name: follow _refs
protocol.description: _refs field lists all related entities. Read them to expand context. No grep needed — _refs IS the index.

protocol.name: reverse lookup via links
protocol.description: Only if step 2 is insufficient. Check link objects to find 'who references me'.

protocol.name: fallback grep
protocol.description: Last resort. Full list + keyword match.

key_concept: _refs is bidirectional. When A references B, B also has A in its _refs. Reading any node gives you its full neighborhood.
anti_pattern: Do NOT grep/search all objects first. Always start with exact read + _refs traversal. Grep is O(N), graph walk is O(degree).
